name: Natilla (Spanish Custard)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the evaporated milk, fresh milk, salt, and lime rind in a heavy saucepan. Bring to a boil over medium heat and set aside to cool.
2. In a mixing bowl, beat the egg yolks until lemon colored.
3. In a separate bowl, mix the cornstarch with enough of the cooled milk to make a light paste. Slowly add the rest of the milk and the egg yolks, stirring constantly.
4. Strain this mixture into another saucepan. Stir in the sugar and cook over low heat, constantly stirring until the mixture thickens. Stir in the vanilla and set aside to cool.
5. Spoon the custard into a serving bowl and refrigerate it until chilled.
